content.html_value:
Philosophy

We will begin with the most general definition of philosophy, as mentioned by the Britannica Encyclopædia (Philosophy, Britannica Encyclopædia):

  • "Philosophy, (from Greek, by way of Latin, philosophia, “love of wisdom”) the rational, abstract, and methodical consideration of reality as a whole or of fundamental dimensions of human existence and experience. Philosophical inquiry is a central element in the intellectual history of many civilizations."

Despite this definition, when we speak of philosophy we are not dealing with something closed. We generally speak of different philosophical systems, often separated according to cultures (Philosophy, Britannica Encyclopædia ).

In this section of History Tracers, we intend to present different philosophical views, which are interconnected with scientific knowledge, in order to show distinct perspectives — which are not wrong for being different — and that help to explain what we experience.

value.name: I understand it as...
value.desc: In this text, we begin to explore the understanding of philosophy.
